title: Pyrus dacica
taxonomy: Pyrus dacica is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family comprises a wide range of plants commonly known as the rose family. Pyrus dacica belongs to the genus Pyrus, a group of deciduous trees and shrubs that primarily produce edible fruits, such as pears. The species Pyrus dacica is native to a specific region and is closely related to other Pyrus species. It is essential to note that the classification may be subject to change based on ongoing botanical research and advancements in the field.
properties: Pyrus dacica, also known as the Dacian pear, is a lesser-known species of pear tree native to the Balkan Peninsula. parts: Pyrus dacica is an extinct species of pear tree. However, I can provide information on the medicinal parts of the related Pyrus communis species.
